plan_adherence_notes: All 8 plan steps were executed exactly as specified: (1) regex patterns for og:image and 4 twitter:* tags added, (2) build_market_preview() updated with outcomes array, og_description, and image field, (3) render_frontend_index_html() injects og:image and all twitter:* tags, (4) index.html has default placeholder tags, (5) og-image.png created at frontend/public/, (6) MarketPreview TS interface updated, (7) comprehensive unit + integration tests added, (8) full suite passes (290/290). Minor deduction: og:description is replaced twice in render_frontend_index_html() — first with plain description (line 884), then overwritten with probability-enriched og_description (line 906). Functionally correct but redundant work.
missed_questions: Should og:image:width and og:image:height meta tags be included? Social platforms use these to reserve space before image loads.
What are the actual dimensions/content of the placeholder og-image.png? At 3.6KB it's very minimal — could be inadequate for social previews.
Should the first og:description replacement (line 884) be removed now that it's superseded by the probability-enriched version (line 906)?
reason: Implementation closely follows the plan derived from thorough research. All 8 steps completed. Key deliverables verified: (1) outcomes array with probabilities in build_market_preview and /v1/markets/{id}/meta endpoint, (2) og:description includes live probabilities formatted as 'Yes: 65% | No: 35% — description', (3) og:image tag with static placeholder, (4) full Twitter Card support (twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image), (5) index.html has default tags for regex replacement, (6) TypeScript interface updated, (7) 7 new tests covering unit and integration scenarios including multi-outcome markets. All 290 tests pass. Code uses proper html.escape with quote=True for XSS prevention. The redundant og:description replacement is a minor code smell but not a correctness issue. The 3.6KB placeholder image is adequate for MVP — can be improved later.
